name: MeSH 2023 - New Headings with Scope Notes
description: This dataset includes all newly added MeSH Main Headings for 2023. Headings play a central role in MeSH vocabulary as a unit of Indexing and retrieval. With the exception of Class 3 Descriptors, all descriptors are organized into a numbered tree structure or hierarchy that allows users to browse in a orderly fashion from broader to narrower topics. Scope Notes provide a concise summary describing the meaning and scope of the MeSH Descriptor.

columns.name: pillbox_score
columns.description: [SOURCE: PILLBOX/NLM] If an image exists and splscore does not match the score value of the pill in the image, NLM adds a score value to improve search.

columns.name: pillbox_imprint
columns.description: [SOURCE: PILLBOX/NLM] If an image exists and the imprint text does not match splimprint, NLM adds the imprint presented on the image to improve the search experience.  For a small number of pills, the imprint has changed.  In those situations, NLM will include the previous imprint text.

description: This resource was retired on January 28, 2021 and is no longer updated. These data remain available to support research and development efforts. Pillbox's final image library is available at https://ftp.nlm.nih.gov/projects/pillbox/pillbox_production_images_full_202008.zip. For more information on Pillbox's retirement visit https://www.nlm.nih.gov/pubs/techbull/ja20/ja20_pillbox_discontinue.html. Pillbox contains metadata for oral solid dosage form medications, derived from FDA drug labeling, in

name: MeSH Publication Types
description: (Last updated - 2023) Publication Types (Publication Characteristics) are Descriptors that indicate what an indexed item is, (i.e., its genre, rather than what it is about - for example, Historical Article). They may include Publication Components, such as Charts; Publication Formats, such as Editorial; and Study Characteristics, such as Clinical Trial. They function as metadata, rather than being about the content. These records are searchable in PubMed as Publication Type [PT], and the terms i

name: PubMed total records by publication year
description: Yearly citation totals from each year of the MEDLINE/PubMed Baseline referencing citations back to year 1781. These totals may increase over time for a particular year as new citations are added. For example, 25 citations were listed for the year 1800 in the 2018 MEDLINE/PubMed Baseline, while the 2019 Baseline includes 387 citations for that year.

name: Health Services Research Projects in Progress (HSRProj) - Archived Data
description: Health Services Research Projects in Progress (HSRProj) was a database of health services research and public health projects in progress, related to research in quality, cost, and access to health care. It included behavioral health research and public health research, with over 38,000 projects and information back to the 1990s. This resource was retired on September 14, 2021 and is no longer updated.

name: MeSH Qualifiers with Scope Notes
description: Listed below are MeSH Topical Qualifiers listed by Name, Abbreviation, and Short Form. Each Qualifier is defined by a Scope Note that provides guidance on how it should be used.

name: Disaster Lit - Archived Data
provider: National Library of Medicine, National Institutes of Health
description: This resource was retired on June 1, 2021 and is no longer updated. These data remain available to support research and development efforts. Disaster Lit®: Database for Disaster Medicine and Public Health is a dataset of links to disaster medicine and public health documents available on the Internet at no cost. Documents include expert guidelines, research reports, conference proceedings, training classes, factsheets, websites, databases, and similar materials selected from over 700 organizatio

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Add Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Add Report lists new Descriptors and Supplementary Concept Records (SCRs) that have been added to MeSH for the upcoming new release. It also includes terms that replace deleted terms, as well as new entry combinations (new precoordinated descriptor heading that replaces an existing descriptor/subheading combination). This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MEDLINE/PubMed Baseline Statistics: Min/Max Report
description: *A file containing all Min/Max Baseline Reports for 2005-2023 in their original format is available in the Attachments section below. A second file includes a separate set of reports, made available from 2002-2017, that did not include OLDMEDLINE records.* MEDLINE/PubMed annual statistical reports are based upon the data elements in the baseline versions of MEDLINE®/PubMed are available. For each year covered the reports include: total citations containing each element; total occurrences of each

name: MEDLINE/PubMed Baseline Statistics: Misc Report
description: *A file containing all Misc Baseline Reports for 2018-2023 in their original format is available in the Attachments section below.* MEDLINE/PubMed annual statistical reports are based upon the data elements in the baseline versions of MEDLINE®/PubMed are available. For each year covered the reports include: total citations containing each element; total occurrences of each element; minimum/average/maximum occurrences of each element in a record; minimum/average/maximum length of a single element

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Combine Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Combine Report lists new Entry Combinations. These are cases where a new, precoordinated Descriptor has been created to replace an existing Descriptor / Qualifier combination. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Preferred Term Update Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Preferred Term Update Report lists changes to the Preferred Term of a Descriptor or a Supplementary Concept Record (SCR). This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Delete Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Delete Report lists Descriptors and Supplementary Concept Records (SCRs) that have been removed from MeSH. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: FDA Notices of Judgment Collection, 1908-1966
provider: US National Library of Medicine, National Institutes of Health

columns.name: ocr.fulltext
columns.description: Vertical lines (pipe characters) represent line breaks in the original Notice of Judgement documents.

tags: united states food and drug administration
description: A digital archive of the published federal notices of judgment (a summary of the final outcome of a court case) for manufacturers and products prosecuted under authority of the 1906 Pure Food and Drug Act (https://fdanj.nlm.nih.gov/)

name: Health Services and Sciences Research Resources (HSRR) - Archived Data
description: Health Services and Sciences Research Resources (HSRR) provided information about research datasets and instruments/indices employed in Health Services Research, Behavioral and Social Sciences and Public Health. This resource was retired on September 14, 2021 and is no longer updated. It is provided for research and historical relevance.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Merge Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) NOTE - There were no Merges for 2025 MeSH. The MeSH 2026 Update - Merge Report describes cases where two or more terms have merged into a single term. The term(s) being merged into another term become(s) an Entry Term. Merges can be between Descriptors and Supplementary Concept Records (SCRs), between Descriptors, or between SCRs. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: Health Services Research Information Central (HSRIC) - Archived Data
description: Health Services Research Information Central (HSRIC) was a web portal and current awareness service of information on health services research. HSRIC was retired on September 14, 2021 and is no longer updated. HSRIC included information on meetings, webinars, new web-born reports (analyses, statistics), datasets, and general news. These data are provided to support research and for historical relevance.

name: Learning Resources Database
tags: united states national library of medicine
description: The Learning Resources Database is a catalog of interactive tutorials, videos, online classes, finding aids, and other instructional resources on National Library of Medicine (NLM) products and services. Resources may be available for immediate use via a browser or downloadable for use in course management systems.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Replace Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Replace Report lists Descriptors and Supplementary Concept Records (SCRs) where an existing MeSH term has been replaced by another term. Note that replacement terms are not necessarily new to the vocabulary; terms are sometimes consolidated under or moved to other existing terms. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Update All Fields Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Update (All Fields) Report lists each individual modification made to a Descriptor or Supplementary Concept Record (SCR). More than one field on a term can be modified during YEP, resulting in a term having multiple entries on the list. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH 2026 Update - Registry Number Update Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 - 2026 changes) The MeSH 2026 Update - Registry Number Update Report lists changes to Registry Numbers on a Descriptor or Supplementary Concept Record (SCR). This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH 2024 Update - Split Report
description: (Includes MeSH 2023 changes) The MeSH 2024 Update - Split Report lists terms that are replaced by a set of terms, either descriptors or SCRs, instead of a single term. This report includes MeSH changes from previous years, starting from 2023.

name: MeSH Population Groups (Type 5 SCR)
description: Working with partners across NIH, led by NIMHD and the NLM OBSSR-Behavioral Ontology Working Group, MeSH on November 29, 2022 added Federally recognized American Indian and Alaskan Native (AI/AN) tribal names and ethnic/ethnolinguistic minority terms as newly created type 5 SCR designated as “Population Groups”. These minority names (1,700+ terms) were mapped and reviewed by subject matter experts and scientists within NIH and from outside including Network of the National Library of Medicine me

name: Partners in Information Access for the Public Health Workforce (PHPartners) - Archived Data
description: PHPartners was retired on September 14, 2021 and is no longer updated. Partners in Information Access for the Public Health Workforce (PHPartners) is a web portal and current awareness service of information for the public health workforce. Alerts the communities to meetings, webinars, new web-born reports (analyses, statistics), datasets, and general news. Currently contains over 4,000 items.

name: Profiles in Science

columns.name: Preparer
columns.description: A person or corporate body that prepared the resource

columns.name: Speaker
columns.description: A person or corporate body that presented in the resource

columns.name: Reviewer
columns.description: A person or corporate body that assessed the resource for any necessary changes

columns.name: Related Material NLM ID(s)
columns.description: NLM Identifier of related item(s). Mainly used when the original source is segmented into multiple items

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description: For correspondence only (letters and memoranda) the name of the person or corporate body to whom the resource is addressed

columns.name: Publisher
columns.description: A person or corporate body that prepared and issued the original resource

columns.name: Permission Statement
columns.description: Specific rights statements and permissions for the source

columns.name: Periodical
columns.description: Main title of the publication (such as a journal, newspaper, or magazine) where the source was issued as one of a consecutively numbered and continued series

columns.name: Original Repository
columns.description: For resources not held at the National Library of Medicine, the name of the institution focused on the care and storage of the original resource

columns.name: Sponsor
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference that  supported the creation of the resource

columns.name: Story Section
columns.description: Profiles in Science collection "Story" section title (exhibit section)

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: Applied subject terms from Medical Subject Headings (MeSH)

columns.name: Transcriber
columns.description: A person or corporate body that transcribed the resource

columns.name: Translator
columns.description: A person or corporate body that translated the resource

columns.name: Unknown Contributor
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference with an unknown role or association with the resource

columns.name: Narrator
columns.description: A person or corporate body that provided accompanying commentary in the resource

columns.name: Lederberg Grouping
columns.description: Donor-designated sub-series for the Joshua Lederberg Profiles in Science Collection

columns.name: Language
columns.description: The language of the intellectual content of the digital object

columns.name: Interviewer
columns.description: A person or corporate body that interviewed someone for the resource

columns.name: Interviewee
columns.description: A person or corporate body interviewed for the resource

columns.name: Genre
columns.description: The nature or genre of the content of the source.  Terms from the Art and Architecture Thesaurus (AAT)

columns.name: Extent (value)
columns.description: The size of the item, often used to note the number of pages

columns.name: Extent (type)
columns.description: The size of the item, often used to note the number of pages

columns.name: Editor
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference that determines the final content of a resource

columns.name: Date (year)
columns.description: Approximate or exact year associated with the material. Standard format of YYYY

columns.name: Date
columns.description: The date an item was created or first appeared (i.e., when a letter was written, interview recorded, article published, speech delivered, or photograph taken). The Date of an item can be (i) Date Unknown (ii) a definite year, month, and day (iii) an inclusive date (a span of time) or (iv) a conjectured (probable, approximate) date. No standard format

columns.name: Creator
columns.description: A person or corporate body primarily responsible for making the content of the resource

columns.name: Conference
columns.description: The title of a meeting of the representatives of organizations (i.e., corporate bodies) or of individuals to discuss or act on topics of common interest

columns.name: Author of Introduction
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference that wrote the introduction to the material

columns.name: Annotator
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference that added notes to the resource

columns.name: Annotation
columns.description: Reflective notes provided by donor of the collection about specific items. Only available for the Joshua Lederberg and Oswald T. Avery Profiles in Science collections

columns.name: Title
columns.description: A title expression for the digital object. May be transcribed from the object, or inherited from a source resource/resource component record, or provided by the repository.

columns.name: Profiles Collection
columns.description: Name of Profiles in Science Collection

columns.name: Photographer
columns.description: A person or corporate body that took photographs in the resource

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Context or written representation of the resource

columns.name: Format
columns.description: The basic content type of the resource

columns.name: Moderator
columns.description: A person or corporate body that presided over an assembly, meeting, or discussion in the resource

columns.name: Collection ID
columns.description: Unique NLM identifier for the Profiles in Science collection

columns.name: Rights
columns.description: Standardized rights statements for the source

columns.name: NLM ID
columns.description: Unique identifier for the item. Organized as collection identifierXitem

columns.name: Illustrator
columns.description: A person or corporate body that drew or created pictures or images for the resource

columns.name: Funder
columns.description: A person, corporate body, or conference that provided funding for the resource

columns.name: Full Text
columns.description: Entire text of the source. Some text is manually transcribed, most is automatically generated by OCR

description: Profiles in Science presents the lives and work of innovators in science, medicine, and public health through in-depth research, curation, and digitization of archival collection materials. This dataset includes full text and metadata describing nearly 30,000 items from Profiles in Science, including digitized correspondence, photographs, laboratory notebooks, diaries, and more that provide insight into the challenges and successes of scientific discovery and the variety of roles, paths, and per
